title: SuperBPE

paper.title: SuperBPE: Space Travel for Language Models

question: Does SuperBPE's superword tokenization reproduce the encoding-efficiency gains at small scale on a Mac - SuperBPE encoding more bytes per token than BPE, BPE's efficiency plateauing while SuperBPE keeps rising with vocab size, and an interior-optimal transition point?
kitFit: Tokenizer-only change: swap the tokenizer at the kit's setup_tokenizer() and re-measure bytes/token and (small-model) bits-per-byte in the same harness. The transition-point sweep is a clean VoidSpark autonomous-experiment target.
nextAction: Tokenizer claims reproduced (C2/C3/C4/C6) and written up in REPRO_REPORT.md; 12-slide deck ready. Remaining (optional): the noisy small-model BPB sweep, and recording the video.

gpuBudget.pricingNote: This reproduction is tokenizer-level: the core claims need NO GPU. Tokenizer sweeps run on CPU; the optional small-model t-sweep runs locally on Apple MPS. Estimated cloud spend: $0.

stages.label: Encoding-efficiency sweeps (BPE vs SuperBPE vs naive)
stages.gate: BPE bytes/token plateaus with vocab while SuperBPE keeps rising; transition-point curve has an interior optimum.

stages.label: Small-model t-sweep, matched training bytes
stages.gate: BPE and SuperBPE train stably to comparable bits-per-byte at matched compute (direction only; downstream out of scope at this scale).

video.workingTitle: Tokenizers That Span Words: I Reproduced SuperBPE on a Laptop
video.definitionOfDone: The video shows the two-phase superword mechanism, the from-scratch code, the reproduced encoding-efficiency curves (BPE plateau vs SuperBPE), the transition-point sweep, and an honest note that the 8B MMLU gain is out of scope at this scale.
